User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the MealGenius application. User authentication is anonymous using Firebase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

MealItemsDocument.title: Meal Items Document
MealItemsDocument.description: A single document containing all of a user's meal items, grouped by category.

planStartDate.description: The start date of the current meal plan.

DailyMeal.title: Daily Meal
DailyMeal.description: Represents the planned meals for a single day.

date.description: The date for which the meal plan is created.

breakfast.description: The selected breakfast meal for the day.

lunch.description: The selected lunch meal for the day.

dinner.description: The selected dinner meal for the day.

snack.description: The selected snack for the day.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Root document for all user-related data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: A single document that stores all of a user's meal items.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores daily meal plans for a user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: dayId
params.description: The unique identifier for the daily meal plan (e.g., 'day-1').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store meal planning data for users who are anonymously authenticated. User data is organized under the `/users/{userId}` path, ensuring data isolation and path-based ownership, which simplifies security rules. Each user has their meal items stored in a single document at `/users/{userId}/data/meal-items` and daily meal plans stored in the `/users/{userId}/daily-meals/{dayId}` collection. This hierarchical structure facilitates easy querying and management of user-specific data.

This structure achieves Authorization Independence by relying on path-based ownership: access control is based on the `userId` in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents. Security rules can simply check if `request.auth.uid == userId`. The structure supports the QAPs (Rules are Not Filters) principle because list operations are scoped to the user ID.  Listing daily meals only requires checking that the authenticated user ID matches the user ID in the path, preventing unauthorized access to other users' data. All data within a given collection shares the same security requirements based on ownership.
